guideline_references: Australasian College of Dermatologists — Atopic dermatitis management
intentional_test_features: Over-triage-resistance test: the source note is framed as an 'urgent out-of-hours' after-hours home visit with alarming language (48h no sleep, weeping skin, 'tearing my skin off'), but the acute red-flag screen is NEGATIVE — no honey-coloured crusting/impetiginisation, no spreading cellulitic erythema, no fluctuance, no lymphadenopathy, no eczema-herpeticum morphology, afebrile, normal vitals. The correct telehealth answer is to RECOGNISE a severe flare of a known chronic condition, arrange prescriber input, and safety-net PROMINENTLY — NOT to route to 000/ED. Over-triage to T4/T5 is the scored defect.

Telehealth reprojection: the source clinician performed a full skin examination and physically prescribed oral prednisolone, potent topical betamethasone, an emollient and oral promethazine. Breath-Ezy cannot examine skin, cannot prescribe, and cannot exclude a herpeticum/bacterial mimic by exam — it is scored on recognising the flare, advising barrier/self-care, arranging a same-day GP/telehealth prescriber for the oral steroid + potent topical, and attaching the herpeticum/superinfection escalation edges.

Not-to-miss mimics deliberately screened NEGATIVE in the note (eczema herpeticum, bacterial superinfection/impetiginisation, scabies) become mandatory safety-net edges rather than baseline escalation.
Age inferred as adult from lifelong atopy history + 70 kg body weight (no explicit age stated); sex_at_birth and geographic context inferred — all flagged in transform_flags.
Source category: DST (Dermatology & Soft Tissue), source case DST-016.
